Definitely related to your experience with anxiety. Started with me in my middle to late 20's. Was put on beta blockers, had numerous cardio tests, etc. I too worked, shopped and had to function. I believe it intensified coming up to, and through, menopause. Numerous trips to ER thinking 'heart attack'. Had a severe panic attack whilst driving to work. Beautiful day, no worries and all of a sudden I knew I was going to die. Had to pull off a major highway and call for a tow truck to take me home. Humiliating. Fear of having another attack is crippling. But as you say, one has to just 'carry on' and work through it. It's the only way to beat it.

Pinned to the bed, every muscle clenched, I tried taking in huge gulps of air. It wasn't enough: I couldn't breathe.

A long-lost episode of The Morecambe and Wise show, broadcast just once almost sixty years ago, has been unearthed - and is returning to our screens. Thought to have been lost forever, it will be broadcast on the BBC and uploaded to iPlayer in May, to commemorate one hundred years since Eric Morecambe was born. Set in a nudist colony, the episode features British actress Ann Hamilton, and Jenny Lee-Wright plays Morecambe's niece.

The King and Queen have laid a bouquet of flowers and a handwritten note at the site of the 9/11 terror attacks in New York, on the third day of their US state visit. On the first stop of a packed and varied itinerary, they paid their respects to the victims almost 25 years on at the National September 11 Memorial, where the World Trade Center stood. It is Charles and Camilla's first visit to the memorial, which opened in 2011 and serves as a permanent place of remembrance.

The Queen joined a starry reception for New York's literary crowd on Wednesday, touring an exhibit of Anglo-American items with the actress Sarah Jessica Parker. Camilla was also reunited with Dame Anna Wintour, Global Editorial Director of Vogue, who removed her trademark sunglasses to greet the royal visitor with kisses. The Queen, in a navy blue crepe silk dress and coat by Fiona Clare, with a Britannia red, white and blue brooch that belonged to the late Queen, was at the New York Public Library for the event hosted by her charity, the Queen's Reading Room.

A new statue in central London has sparked a debate over whether it could be the capital's latest Banksy. The mystery figure sprang up in Waterloo Place in central London in the early hours on Wednesday, apparently with the legendary artist's signature, 'Banksy', at the bottom - although the artist has not yet confirmed whether it is his. It depicts a man marching off the end of a plinth while holding a flag entirely covering his face and is located along Pall Mall, near to the Athenaeum Club and the Crimean War Memorial.

A new Netflix drama will portray how Rachel Nickell's toddler son was taken out of Britain by his father to begin a secret life abroad away from his mother's killer. Miss Nickell, 23, was stabbed 49 times on Wimbledon Common in front of her two-year-old son Alex Hanscombe as they walked their dog Molly on July 15, 1992.   • Job done. Now he can follow the path that Saint Marcus trod.
  • Champions League semi-final and right on cue this paper produces a negative Arsenal story.
  • Tonight, the football will be atrocious.
  • Got got got got got need got got got. Simpler times. And when you got a foily you felt like you won the lottery..
  • Fantastic game last night, by two top teams, really good football and end to end game play. Get ready to fall asleep tonight, watching the Bottlers get beat. Snooze ball awaits.
  • Shouldn’t be going anywhere but back to OT. How much was Grealish and he’s not half the player. Time served move on
